Package org.cibseven.connect.ai.agent

Interface AgentResponse

All Superinterfaces:
ConnectorResponse
All Known Implementing Classes:
AgentResponseImpl
public interface AgentResponse extends ConnectorResponse
Response interface for the LangChain4j agent connector.

After a successful execution the following output parameter is available for BPMN output mapping:

  • output — the final text response produced by the agent
  • outputAiMeta — EU AI Act Art. 50(2) marker. A Map<String,Object> carrying aiGenerated=true, runId, provider, model, responseId, and generatedAt. Mapped as a sibling of output (typically named <outputVar>_aiMeta) so downstream Human Tasks, gateways, and audit consumers can detect AI-generated content without parsing the answer text
  • memoryId — identifier of the chat-memory entry used by this invocation (only populated when useChatMemory was active); persist this value into a process variable to continue the same conversation on a subsequent invocation, e.g. after a human task

The chat log is not returned via the response: the connector writes/updates a process variable named AGENT_CONNECTOR_LOG_PREFIX + <activityId> directly during the invocation.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    String
    getMemoryId()
    Returns the chat memory identifier used during this invocation, or null when chat memory was not activated.
    String
    getOutput()
    Returns the final text response produced by the agent.
    Map<String,Object>
    getOutputAiMeta()
    Returns the EU AI Act Art.

    Methods inherited from interface org.cibseven.connect.spi.ConnectorResponse

    getResponseParameter, getResponseParameters

  • Method Details

    • getOutput

      String getOutput()
      Returns the final text response produced by the agent.

    • getOutputAiMeta

      Map<String,Object> getOutputAiMeta()
      Returns the EU AI Act Art. 50(2) marker describing the AI-generated nature of getOutput(), or null when no marker could be assembled (e.g. the agent returned no successful response).

    • getMemoryId

      String getMemoryId()
      Returns the chat memory identifier used during this invocation, or null when chat memory was not activated.
